name.description: Different formats of the app name for use in different contexts.

alphanumeric.description: A **filesafe name** for the app consisting only of alphanumeric characters (A-z, and 0-9), e.g. 'MyApp123'. Will be used mainly for file names, e.g. 'MyApp123.xcodeproj'.

display_name.description: The **display name** of your app, e.g. 'My App 123' or 'šŅļ„Āģ„āĘ„Éó„É™'. Accepts any string. Will be used as the app name on the iOS/Android home screen, in macOS Finder, and in Windows Explorer.

reverse_dns.description: The **reverse-Domain Name Specifier** for your app, e.g. 'com.example.my-app-123'. Accepts alphanumeric characters (A-z and 0-9), hyphens, and underscores. Will be used to fill in `android.package_namespace`, `android.application_id`, `windows.namespace`, `ios.bundle_identifier`, and `macos.bundle_identifier`. Hyphens and underscores will be sanitised as appropriate for these destinations, so don't worry which you use here.

android.description: Android-specific overrides for the default values (which are based on `name`).

application_id.description: The **application ID** used on the Play Store, e.g. 'com.example.my_app_123'. Accepts only alphanumeric characters (A-z and 0-9), and underscores. Corresponds to `android.defaultConfig.applicationId` in `app/build.gradle`. Recommended to match `android.package_namespace`.

package_namespace.description: The **package namespace**, e.g. 'com.example.my_app_123'. Accepts only alphanumeric characters (A-z and 0-9), and underscores. Corresponds to `android.namespace` in `app/build.gradle`. Recommended to match `android.application_id`.

root_project_name.description: The **display name** of your app, e.g. 'My App 123' or 'šŅļ„Āģ„āĘ„Éó„É™'. Accepts any string. Corresponds to `rootProject.name` in `settings.gradle`. Will be used as the app name on the Android home screen.

ios.description: iOS-specific overrides for the default values (which are based on `name`).

bundle_display_name.description: The **display name** of your app, e.g. 'My App 123' or 'šŅļ„Āģ„āĘ„Éó„É™'. Accepts any string. Corresponds to the `CFBundleDisplayName` key in the `Info.plist` file. Will be used as the app name on the iOS home screen.

bundle_identifier.description: The **bundle identifier** of your app, e.g. 'com.example.my-app-123'. Accepts only alphanumeric characters (A-z and 0-9), and hyphens. Corresponds to the `PRODUCT_BUNDLE_IDENTIFIER` Xcode build variable, used to fill in the `CFBundleIdentifier` key in the `Info.plist` file.

macos.description: macOS-specific overrides for the default values (which are based on `name`).

bundle_display_name.description: The **display name** of your app, e.g. 'My App 123' or 'šŅļ„Āģ„āĘ„Éó„É™'. Accepts any string. Corresponds to the `PRODUCT_BUNDLE_IDENTIFIER` Xcode build variable, used to fill in the `CFBundleDisplayName` key in the `Info.plist` file. Will be used as the app name in macOS Finder.

bundle_identifier.description: The **bundle identifier** of your app, e.g. 'com.example.my-app-123'. Accepts only alphanumeric characters (A-z and 0-9), and hyphens. Corresponds to the `PRODUCT_BUNDLE_IDENTIFIER` Xcode build variable, used to fill in the `CFBundleIdentifier` key in the `Info.plist` file.

react_native_version.description: The `{major}.{minor}` version of `react-native` to align to, e.g. '0.82'. Defaults to the highest mutually supported across both `react-native-macos` and `react-native-windows`.

windows.description: Windows-specific overrides for the default values (which are based on `name`).

display_name.description: The **display name** of your app, e.g. 'My App 123' or 'šŅļ„Āģ„āĘ„Éó„É™'. Accepts any string. Corresponds to the `ProjectName` value in the `.vcxproj` file. Will be used as the app name in Windows Explorer.

namespace.description: The WinRT and C++ **namespace**, e.g. 'com.example.myapp123'. Accepts only alphanumeric characters (A-z and 0-9). When used in C++, `.` characters are converted to `::`, e.g. 'com::example::myapp123'.

project_name.description: A **filesafe name** for the app consisting only of alphanumeric characters (A-z, and 0-9), e.g. 'MyApp123'. Will be used mainly for file names, e.g. 'MyApp123.vcxproj'.
